original_hypothesis.title: setLiquidationPair Grants Unnecessary Max Approval of Underlying Asset to LP
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When the owner calls `setLiquidationPair`, the function approves `type(uint256).max` of the underlying asset (e.g., USDC) to the new LP address. The liquidation flow only requires the LP to provide prize tokens, never to pull underlying assets. A compromised or malicious LP contract could drain all underlying assets from the vault via transferFrom.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The LP's role is to provide prize tokens to the PrizePool and receive vault shares. The underlying asset approval is unnecessary for this flow but grants the LP unlimited access to the vault's underlying asset balance, including idle assets and potentially assets in transit during deposits.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _asset.safeApprove(address(liquidationPair_), type(uint256).max) — approves underlying asset, not prize token

preconditions: LP contract has functionality to call transferFrom on the vault's underlying asset
Owner sets LP to a vulnerable or malicious contract

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Max approval of underlying asset to LP; LP should never need underlying asset access
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: If LP contract has any vulnerability, vault's underlying assets can be drained
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Approval should be to prize token, not underlying asset; or should not exist at all

analysis_questions: Does the LP ever need underlying asset approval for normal operation?
What is the LP's interface — does it expose any function that could pull underlying assets?
Is there any idle underlying balance in the vault that could be drained?
validation_requirements: Verify LP never calls transferFrom on underlying asset in normal flow
Confirm that a malicious LP can call transferFrom to drain vault

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: 1. Owner sets LP to a malicious or compromised contract. 2. LP calls asset.transferFrom(vault, attacker, vault_asset_balance). 3. All underlying assets in the vault are drained, including any idle balance and potentially yield vault shares.
prerequisites: Owner sets malicious LP or LP contract is compromised

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete drain of all underlying assets held by the vault
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All vault depositors lose their principal
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: None — assets transferred out of vault

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test verifying LP cannot access underlying assets

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Any time after LP is set

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
